posts.title: The Precipice
posts.excerpt: Toby Ord's comprehensive analysis of existential risks facing humanity and how we can navigate the challenges of the 21st century. A philosophical and practical guide to safeguarding humanity's future.

posts.title: Superintelligence
posts.excerpt: Nick Bostrom's seminal work on the paths, dangers, and strategies for artificial general intelligence. Explores the potential for AI to surpass human intelligence and the existential risks involved.

posts.title: The Scaling Era
posts.excerpt: Dwarkesh Patel's in-depth interviews with leading AI researchers including Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei, DeepMind cofounder Demis Hassabis, and OpenAI cofounder Ilya Sutskever. A comprehensive portrait of large language models and a vision of what comes next.

posts.title: Situational Awareness: The Decade Ahead
posts.excerpt: Leopold Aschenbrenner's comprehensive analysis of the path from GPT-4 to AGI to superintelligence. A deep dive into compute scaling, intelligence explosions, and the geopolitical implications of the AI race.

posts.title: The upcoming GPT-3 moment for RL
posts.excerpt: RL will soon have its own GPT-3 moment. Rather than fine-tuning models on a small number of environments, we expect the field will shift toward massive-scale training across thousands of diverse environments.

posts.title: The future of AI is already written
posts.excerpt: To a first approximation, the future course of civilization has already been fixed, predetermined by hard physical constraints combined with unavoidable economic incentives. Full automation is inevitable.

posts.title: Life after work
posts.excerpt: Exploring what comes after full automation of human labor.

posts.title: ArXiv Paper 2501.00656
posts.excerpt: Research paper from arXiv. Check the full paper for details.

posts.title: Speculative Decoding
posts.excerpt: A clever technique to speed up LLM inference by producing multiple tokens per forward pass. Explains the algorithm, its derivation, and newer variants.

posts.title: The Mythical Man-Month
posts.excerpt: Fred Brooks' classic essay on software engineering management. Why adding manpower to a late software project makes it later.

posts.title: How Complex Systems Fail
posts.excerpt: Richard Cook's insightful paper on the nature of complex system failures and why they're inevitable.

posts.title: You Are Not Google
posts.excerpt: A reminder that most engineering teams don't need Google-scale solutions. Build what you actually need.

posts.title: The Architecture of Open Source Applications
posts.excerpt: Deep dives into the architecture of successful open source projects. Learn from the best.
